homepage.title: Meet Sarah Dyke - Glastonbury and Somerton Liberal Democrats
homepage.meta_description: Sarah is the best choice to be Glastonbury and Somerton's new MP
headings: h2: Sarah Dyke MP
h3: The local champion for Glastonbury and Somerton
homepage.text_excerpt: Meet Sarah Dyke - Glastonbury and Somerton Liberal Democrats Skip to main content Glastonbury and Somerton Liberal Democrats Sign up! What's on in Glastonbury and Somerton? Volunteer Campaigns Meet Sarah Dyke News Contact us Donate Join Donate Join Volunteer Join the Liberal Democrats Campaigns Sarah’s Household Waste Recycling Centres Petition Save the River Brue Stop Lloyd's Bank from closing Business Rates Relief Petition Sarah's Dentistry petition Sarah's health survey Sarah's Rural Connectivity Survey Resident's survey Somerton and Langport train station Local GP Crisis Stop Sewage Dumping Sarah Dyke MP The local champion for Glastonbury and Somerton A strong voice for Somerset Fixing our NHS and cutting the cost of living A positive plan to get a fairer deal for our area People across Glastonbury & Somerton backed local resident Sarah Dyke to become their MP. Somerset is a special place, and Sarah as our MP is a strong voice for our towns and villages. An MP who will always campaign to protect our rural way of life. Sarah lives in the heart of Somerset and her farming family can trace their local roots back over more than 250 years. Sarah loves our area and wants to get things done as our local MP. Sarah said: “This is my home. I grew up locally, my family live here. We’re all so lucky to live in such a special part of the country. But I also know first-hand the issues facing local residents. “A lot of people round here put their faith in the Conservatives but have been let down. “The NHS is in crisis, the cost of living is rocketing, local farmers have been sold out. The Conservatives have been more interested in scandal or fighting each other rather than fighting for us." “We can tell the Government that they need to do more to cut the cost of living. We need urgent action to ensure people can get the treatment they need on the NHS. And we need to do much more to back our local farming industry. “Enough is enough. It’s time that Somerset got a fairer deal. That's what I’m fighting for as our MP. “My priority will always be to do what is best for the people of Glastonbury & Somerton. “I’ve always fought for what I believe in. As our MP I will work every day to get real action on the issues that matter most for people in our community: standing up for farmers, cutting the cost of living and fixing our NHS. “I understand why people are worried about the future for their children and grandchildren. But we can change the direction of the country and get a better future for everyone in Somerset.” Sign up for email updates Oops! Please check the fields below and submit again. pages.text_excerpt: News - Glastonbury and Somerton Liberal Democrats Skip to main content Glastonbury and Somerton Liberal Democrats Sign up! What's on in Glastonbury and Somerton? Volunteer Campaigns Meet Sarah Dyke News Contact us Donate Join Donate Join Volunteer Join the Liberal Democrats Campaigns Sarah’s Household Waste Recycling Centres Petition Save the River Brue Stop Lloyd's Bank from closing Business Rates Relief Petition Sarah's Dentistry petition Sarah's health survey Sarah's Rural Connectivity Survey Resident's survey Somerton and Langport train station Local GP Crisis Stop Sewage Dumping News Liberal Democrat Success in Somerton Somerset Liberal Democrats are delighted to have won the Somerton division by-election, decisively beating the Conservatives. 29 Mar 2024 View Sarah Dyke: Improving local NHS services is my priority Liberal Democrat MP Sarah Dyke has announced she will stand for the new Glastonbury & Somerton constituency at the next election, promising to make fixing local health services her main focus if re-elected. 27 Oct 2023 View Council Leader calls for Government to fix social care funding crisis The Leader of Somerset Council has written to Michael Gove, Secretary of State for the Department of Levelling Up, Housing and Communities (DLUHC), calling for urgent action to fix the social care funding crisis. 17 Nov 2023 View Sarah Dyke calls for two-month cancer treatment guarantee The Liberal Democrats have called for a new legal right for cancer patients to start treatment within 62 days of an urgent referral. 18 Oct 2023 View Liberal Democrat Success in Castle Cary The Somerset Liberal Democrats are delighted to have gained a seat on Somerset Council in Castle Cary after the by-election on Thursday 10th August. This increases their total councillors on Somerset Council to 62 and a majority of 14. 11 Aug 2023 View Sarah Dyke calls on Chancellor to scrap ‘punitive’ cider tax Newly elected Liberal Democrat MP for Somerton and Frome Sarah Dyke has written to the Chancellor calling on him to scrap the ‘punitive’ cider tax set to come into force next week. 1 Aug 2023 View 1 2 2 Includes stories published while Sarah Dyke was Member of Parliament for Somerton and Frome. pages.title: Business Rates Relief Petition - Glastonbury and Somerton Liberal Democrats
headings: h2: Sarah Dyke's Business Rates Relief Petition
pages.text_excerpt: Business Rates Relief Petition - Glastonbury and Somerton Liberal Democrats Skip to main content Glastonbury and Somerton Liberal Democrats Sign up! What's on in Glastonbury and Somerton? Volunteer Campaigns Meet Sarah Dyke News Contact us Donate Join Donate Join Volunteer Join the Liberal Democrats Campaigns Sarah’s Household Waste Recycling Centres Petition Save the River Brue Stop Lloyd's Bank from closing Business Rates Relief Petition Sarah's Dentistry petition Sarah's health survey Sarah's Rural Connectivity Survey Resident's survey Somerton and Langport train station Local GP Crisis Stop Sewage Dumping Sarah Dyke's Business Rates Relief Petition I call on the Government to reverse its disastrous decision to remove Business Rates relief for hospitality this spring. Many of our local pubs, restaurants, cafes, hotels and entertainment venues are already struggling with spiralling costs; removing the current relief will mean their Business Rates will rocket by 40% or more. These businesses don’t just contribute billions to our economy - they create local jobs, support vibrant town centres, and bring people together. They are the backbone of our rural communities. But some will be forced to close, and these vital rural services will be lost. The Government must act to protect our high streets and reconsider this damaging tax. Sarah's Business Rates Relief Petition Sign below to save rural pubs and urge this Government to rethink their damaging business rates increase. Oops! Please check the fields below and submit again.
